titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Albert Memmi Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday March 17, 2025 at 23:17

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In French, with some materials in English, Hebrew, Arabic, German, Italian, Spanish, and Japanese.

did.abstract: The collection consists of writings, correspondence, project files, teaching materials, journals, photographs, printed material, personal papers, and audiovisual and computer media documenting the life and career of French-Tunisian author, critic, and philosopher Albert Memmi.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 95 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of computer files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 96-97 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Librairie Benoit Forgeot on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2021.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Writings, 1936-2020. II. Correspondence, 1940-2019. III. Photographs, circa 1925-2018. IV. Professional and Personal Papers, 1922-2020. V. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1980-2009.

bioghist.head: Albert Memmi (1920-2020)
bioghist.p: Albert Memmi was a Tunisian-born Jewish Berber Francophone writer, critic, and philosopher best known for his 1957 study Portrait du colonisé, précédé du Portrait du colonisateur (translated as The Colonizer and the Colonized ). His other non-fiction works include Portrait d'un Juif (1962, 1966); L'Homme dominé (1973); La Terre intérieure (1976); Le dépendance (1979); Le racisme (1982); Ce que je crois (1985); and Ah, quel bonheur! (1995). His first novel, La statue de sel (1953), with a preface by Albert Camus, was awarded the Fénéon Prize in 1954. Other novels include Agar (1955), Le scorpion (1969), Le désert (1977), and Le pharaon (1988). Memmi also helped found a psychology institute and the independence journals L'Action and Jeune afrique , and he taught at several universities including École pratique des hautes études and École des hautes études commerciales de Paris.

Memmi was born in French colonial Tunisia to Maïra Sarfati and Fradji Memmi. He was educated in French primary schools, Lycée Carnot in Tunis, where he later briefly taught, the University of Algiers, and the Sorbonne in Paris. During the Nazi occupation of Tunisia, Memmi was imprisoned in a labor camp, from which he later escaped. Memmi was expelled from Tunisia after the country declared independence from France in 1956.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Albert Memmi Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of writings, correspondence, project files, teaching materials, journals, photographs, printed material, personal papers, and audiovisual and computer media documenting the life and career of French-Tunisian author, critic, and philosopher Albert Memmi. Included are drafts, notes, research material, reviews, and fan mail related to works by Memmi such as La Libération du juif ; Portrait du colonisé, précédé du portrait du colonisateur ; Portrait du décolonisé arabo-musulman et de quelques autres ; Le racisme ; and Statue de sel . The collection contains extensive notes and drafts for articles, essays, lectures, speeches, interviews, and unpublished works on decolonization, African Americans, women, Jewish identity, North African literature, antisemitism, racism, oppression, happiness, and alcohol dependency. Memmi's journals, dating from 1936 to 2019, document his early life, work, and travels, in particular his brief imprisonment in a Nazi labor camp during World War II, and later his expulsion from Tunisia following independence from France. Among the correspondents in the collection are writers, educators, activists, artists, and politicians including James Baldwin, Simone de Beauvoir, Albert Camus, Mark Chagall, Claude Lévi-Strauss, Shimon Peres, Jean-Paul Sartre, and Elie Wiesel; anti-racist and human rights groups such as Mouvement contre le racisme et pour l'amitié entre les peuples; and figures associated with the Ne?gritude movement, including Alioune Diop, a Senegalese writer, editor, and founder of the journal Présence africaine , and poet Le?opold Se?ndar Senghor, the first president of Senegal.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Albert Memmi Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: Mouvement contre le racisme et pour l'amitié entre les peuples (France)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of drafts, notes, correspondence, and other materials related to books, articles, speeches, lectures, interviews, and various projects of Albert Memmi, as well as journals and agendas.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six subseries: Books, Shorter Works, Journals and Agendas, Other Writings and Projects, Printed Material, and Writings of Others.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated Materials: Writings can also be found in Series IV. Professional and Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of extensive notes, research materials, and corrected autograph manuscript and typescript drafts of nonfiction and fiction works by Memmi, including Agar, La dépendance , L'Homme dominé , La Libération du juif , Portrait du colonisé , Le racisme , and Statue de sel . The subseries additionally contains files of publicity material, clippings, and readers' letters regarding Memmi's books, compiled by press agencies in Paris.

title.part: À contre-courants : Dictionnaire pour s'éviter des errements, complaisances et complicités

did.unittitle: Notes, drafts, research material, and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Technical" notes about the presentation of the book and the illustration of the cover, with tables of contents, and publishers' and readers' letters.

did.unittitle: Printed material, notes, and other research
Printed material, notes, and other research
Printed material, notes, and other research
Printed material, notes, and other research

Complete draft with presentation notes, notification, and table of contents
"Histoire du Docteur" [drafts with several versions of some chapters]
"Manuscrit complet sous chemise titrée Manuscrit définitif, état n°1. Tunis juillet-août 1955" [draft]
Draft with a few variations from published version

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Jacob Tsur, Léon Poliakov, and Claude Roy.

title.part: Ah, quel bonheur! précédé de L'exercice du bonheur

did.unittitle: "Nouvelle seris - inédited en livre - Bonheurs III" [draft]
L'Exercice du bonheur , drafts and notes

title.part: Le buveur et l'amoureux: le prix de la dépendance
La dépendance: esquisse pour un portrait du dépendant

did.unittitle: Notes, drafts, printed material, and correspondence
Notes, drafts, printed material, and correspondence
Notes, drafts, printed material, and correspondence
"Carnets de la dépendance / Livre de la dépendance" [notes]
"Ajouts possibles" [notes, research, and writings for new edition]
Grand prix des lectrices de Elle , readers' letters, and radio calls

title.part: Le désert: ou, La vie et les aventures de Jubair Ouali El-Mammi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originally subtitled "Chronique du royaume du dedans."

did.unittitle: Drafts with some corrections, notes, and clippings
"Chronique du royaume du dedans" or "La Bible de Gourara" [notes and drafts]
"Chronique du royaume du dedans" or "La Bible de Gourara," working copy [notes and drafts]
"Le désert, Poubelle, mais passages non utilisés à comparer" [draft and notes]
Drafts, notes, and map of "Chronique du royaume du dedans" on tracing paper
Dictionnaire critique à l'usage des incrédules , press, clippings, and readers' letters

title.part: L'écriture colorée, ou, Je vous aime en rouge: essai sur une dimension nouvelle de l'écriture, la couleur

did.unittitle: Drafts and notes, with publishing correspondence

title.part: Eloge des femmes : les coplas du jeune homme amoureux

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unfinished collection of poems, enlarged with "Lettres à Pomme."

did.unittitle: Feu sur 40 idées reçues , press, clippings, and readers' letters
Les Français et le racisme , press, clippings, and readers' letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to L'Homme dominé (1968) and L'Homme dominé : le Noir, le colonisé, le prolétaire, le Juif, la femme, le domestique, le racisme (1973).

did.unittitle: "Le livre de l'oppression," drafts, notes, and printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings and printed material on topics including aggression, alienation, cultural identity, language, violence, and domination.

did.unittitle: L'Homme dominé : le Noir, le colonisé, le prolétaire, le Juif, la femme, le domestique, le racisme , draft, corrected
Journal de guerre , press and clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes correspondence with Tunisian Minister of Culture Mongi Bousnina.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts of poems not included in the book.

did.unittitle: "Oranges amères, ou Le mirliton du ciel" [drafts]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Algerian President Abdelaziz Bouteflika regarding an Algerian edition of Portrait du colonisé (2006).

did.unittitle: "1er projet complètement remanié" [notes, drafts, and research material]
"Journal de la rose" [notebooks] and notes on keywords and subjects
Drafts and notes on language and themes
Drafts and notes on language and themes
Drafts and notes on language and themes
"La rose dans le désert" [drafts, notes, and research material]
"La rose dans le désert," drafts, notes, and research material
"La rose dans le désert/ M2. définitif" [draft, corrected]

title.part: Portrait du colonisé, précédé du portrait du colonisateur

did.unittitle: "Notes à ajouter au Portrait" and "IV. Notes"
"Le colonisé entre les myths" [notes and draft]
Readers' letters, signed typescript of testimony by Léopold Sédar Senghor

title.part: Portrait du décolonisé arabo-musulman et de quelques autres

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes some correspondence about an English translation.

did.unittitle: Portrait du juif / Libération du juif , early notes, drafts, printed material, and publishing correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes class notes and essays from Lycée Carnot, and papers related to his scholarship while he was teaching at École pratique des hautes études in the 1960s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Nicole Védrès, Irving Halperin, Roger Ikor, Jacqueline Arnaud, Jacques Brenner, Jacqueline Kahanoff, and Jean Déjeux.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Nicole Védrès, Irving Halperin, Roger Ikor, Jacqueline Arnaud, Jacques Brenner, Jacqueline Kahanoff, and Jean Déjeux.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Memmi's early research on racism.

did.unittitle: Corrected typescript transcription of a radio broadcast

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Set of letters sent by viewers to the directors of the television program "Faire Face" (broadcast in September 1961), sorted by Memmi in two files, which translate into English as "racists," and "racists who do not recognize themselves as such."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, clippings, ephemera, and writings of Memmi, Léopold Sédar Senghor, and others related to topics such as racism, fear or need of others, Négritude, and Black Americans. Also includes an unbound copy of La Prochain fois, le feu by James Baldwin, inscribed by Baldwin and annotated by Memmi.

did.unittitle: Files on psychology, sociology, and other topics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts and offprints of writings by Memmi and others, printed material, ephemera, and notes related to racism and heterophobia, Black identity, and other topics.

title.part: Le Scorpion, ou, La confession imaginaire

did.unittitle: "Le Scorpion M1 utilisé (sauf qqs. passages à revoir)" [draft, corrected]
Draft with author's and editor's notes
Draft, corrected, and book note titled "Chronique du royaume du dedans"
Une société anonyme , press and clippings
Notes and draft for a foreword

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes discussion of Memmi's career as a teacher in Tunis, until the independence of Tunisia.

did.unittitle: Draft, corrected, with prospectus and biographical note

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes with questions and answers about the novel and answers to a radio interview.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes files of reviews from German and American publications.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Geneviève Bonnefoi, Mohammed Mzali, Avraham Elmalih, Helena Strassova, Édouard Roditi, Lucien Lazare, Jean Wahl, Jean Sénac, René Johannet, André Halimi, Jacqueline Kahanoff, Lucien Paye, Jean Amrouche, and Edith Schreiber-Aujame.

title.part: La terre intérieure: entretiens avec Victor Malka

did.unittitle: "Texte initial complet (entretiens avec V. Malka) magnétophone" [draft, corrected in an unidentified hand]
"Exemplaire-machine sur lequel a travaillé l'imprimeur" [draft, corrected in different hands]
Notes, plans, technical remarks, and illustrations
"La vie impossible de Frantz Fanon," notes, drafts, and related materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes bibliographic notes, topics to develop of the chapter "objet et méthode," and notes arranged by chapter.

did.unittitle: Voyage en Argentine , press and clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of notes, drafts, and other materials related to articles, lectures, speeches, forewords, interviews, and debates by Memmi on various topics, including racism, sociology, psychology, happiness, and addiction.

did.unittitle: Albert Memmi : un entretien avec Robert Davies suivi de Itinéraire de l'expérience vécue à la théorie de la domination , drafts, notes, and proofs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and drafts for articles about religion, cultural identity, economy, Marxism, Tunisia, South Africa, and other topics.

did.unittitle: Articles, lectures, and other shorter writings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles for Le Monde and Le Figaro .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes articles, interviews, and lectures in French, English, Italian or Spanish, mainly relating to La dépendance ; clippings; announcements for lectures and symposiums; and typescripts, including a lecture for the 35th anniversary of the Warsaw Ghetto uprising and transcription of the radio broadcast "Radioscopie."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts of writings on Léopold Sédar Senghor and Naïm Kattan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the preface to Anthologie des écrivains maghrébins d'expression française .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings about alcohol addiction, love, nomadism, Tunisia, Judaism and secularism, Zionism, Francophonie, racism, the student condition, and the developing world; and corrected typescripts of articles, speeches, and other shorter works titled "Plea of a Tyrant," "Portrait: Elie Wiesel," "Neapolitan Farce," and "Notes about Albert Camus." Also includes a draft about Albert Cohen, with Cohen's corrections.

did.unittitle: "La citadelle," mockup with illustrations by Gérard Blanchet
"Courte note sur une philosophie de la relation," drafts
Freud et la tradition juive (afterword by Memmi), press and clippings
"A voix nue," typescripts, corrected, for France-Culture radio broadcast with Eliane Contini

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with the publisher Arléa.

did.unittitle: Interviews and shorter works - typescripts and printed versions
Interviews - typescripts, printed versions, and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interview with Jacqueline Leiner and others.

did.unittitle: "Lettre d'un laïc à un ami croyant"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, drafts, and research for an unfinished open letter to the philosopher Guy Coq about the sacred, Christianism, secularism and humanism, totalitarianism, and intolerance.

did.unittitle: "Lettre d'un laïc à un ami croyant"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings on art, love, and time, and a poem dedicated to Albert Bitran.

did.unittitle: Nous, les nègres (foreword by Memmi), press and clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and drafts of writings such as "Antarès, conte policier," "Le miroir fêlé," "L'impossible rencontre," "La broche," "La vengeance," and "Le journal de Don Juan," with a note about le Prix Carthage, won by Memmi in 1953 for La Statue de sel . Some stories were later published in Térésa et autres femmes .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the foreword for Anthologie des écrivains maghrébins , and works published in journals such as Esprit , Temps Modernes , and Revue de l'Institut de sociologie .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and printed material for a lecture during the symposium "Regards croisés sur la francophonie au Maroc."

did.unittitle: Writings on art, literature, psychology, sociology and other subjects
Drafts and photocopies of printed articles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of journals and agendas of Memmi, including the diary he kept while imprisoned in a Nazi labor camp in 1943, and a diary from his voyage to Israel in 1962, as well as a typescript of the book Tunisie, an I , a compilation of his journal entries from 1955-1956.

did.unittitle: "Journal littéraire," typescript printout of journal entries dated 1936-1943
"Notes sur la meillure manière de conduire sa vie"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary and correspondence related to Memmi's imprisonment in a labor camp during the Nazi occupation of Tunisia.

did.unittitle: Tunisie, an I : journal tunisien, 1955-1956 , typescript printout, corrected, with a letter from Guy Dugas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of notes and drafts for books, lectures, articles, and projects, some of which were unrealized. Materials are primarily organized by subject, keyword, or title. Included are files related to Anthologie des écrivains maghrébins d'expression française , edited by Memmi; decolonization and liberation; dependence and dominance; Judaism and judeity; race and racism; and women.

title.part: Anthologie des écrivains maghrébins d'expression française

did.unittitle: Notes, drafts, bibliography, correspondence, and printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes foreword of Le Roman maghrébin by Abdelkabir Khatibi.

did.unittitle: Notes, drafts, printed material, and correspondence related to North African literature

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photograph of Memmi.

did.unittitle: Notes on decolonization for Encyclopædia Universalis
Notes and drafts related to decolonization and liberation
Notes and writings related to decolonization, Africa, and other topics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes essays, book reviews by Memmi for the New York Times , and drafts in English.

did.unittitle: Notes and writings related to decolonization, annotated

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains printed material and other documents related to decolonization, a small amount of correspondence, and reviews of Memmi's work. Includes an article by Cameroonian musician and journalist Francis Bebey on Black music and poetry, and articles on topics such as the American civil rights movement, police violence against African Americans, racism, and desegregation; women's rights in North Africa; apartheid; and Latin American culture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and typescripts for two scenarios, "Deborah," and "Agar," and synopsis for film adaptation of Hamidou Kane's novel, L'Aventure ambigüe .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts of writings by Memmi, notes, clippings, offprints, and ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annotated clippings, catalogs, brochures, offprints, ephemeras of conference proceedings, writings of others, and lectures and interviews by Memmi, as well as correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annotated clippings, catalogs, brochures, offprints, ephemera, typescripts of conference proceedings, writings of others, and lectures and interviews by Memmi, as well as correspondence.

did.unittitle: Notes on Judaism organized by keyword or topic
Notes on Judaism organized by keyword or topic

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes on "auteurs."

did.unittitle: Notes and drafts on various topics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include arts and literature, interracial marriage, collapsing values, Tunisia before and after independence, Israel, and totalitarianism. Also included are drafts of "La maison de vos rêves" for a radio broadcast and an essay about Emperor Nero (Alger, 1945).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include sex education, bilingualism, teacher/student relationships, symbolism in Bergsonian philosophy, and Tunisian Jewish marriage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include opinion, irony, insult, civic education, bargaining, truth, and the question of meaning.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sketches of stories, news, or articles, arranged by theme or title, such as "The metro ticket," "Ordinary madness," "Privileges," "Greed," and "The carnivorous rabbit."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sketches of stories, news, or articles, arranged by theme or title, such as "The metro ticket," "Ordinary madness," "Privileges," "Greed," and "The carnivorous rabbit."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed material, conference ephemera, correspondence, and other documents related to dependence, Jewish identity, secularism, North African literature, racism, decolonization, and philosophy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include dependence and dominance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include love, passion, women, seduction, loyalty, and jealousy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include love, passion, women, seduction, loyalty, and jealousy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes and drafts related to "Le livre nécessaire," "Carnets d'un incrédule," and "Pense-bête."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes and drafts related to "Le livre nécessaire," "Carnets d'un incrédule," and "Pense-bête."

did.unittitle: Notes organized by keyword or topic
Notes organized by keyword or topic
Notes organized by keyword or topic [notebook]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on Franz Kafka's Letter to his Father , with a letter by Memmi to his son Daniel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unfinished project, sometimes titled "Le livre nécessaire," which was planned in three parts or three separate books: "Aphorismes," "Carnets d'un incrédule," and "Échanges."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes on projects related to decolonization, a women's manifesto, love strategy through the ages, and Black writers, as well as the development of Agar .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and materials arranged by keyword about womanhood, women's liberation, Simone de Beauvoir, and other topics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts, notes, printed material, and drawings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ideas, quotes, comments, clippings, and writings organized by keyword, mostly from the 1990s-early 2000s, with some early files on interracial marriage. Subjects include art, astrology, atheism, fundamentalism, magic, numerology, racism, purity, and writing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes about travels in Greece, Portugal, the West Indies, the United States, and Canada, primarily related to decolonization.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes and ephemera related to Indigenous Americans and Asian Americans, incarceration rates in Washington state, and some documents related to the American Israel Public Affairs Committee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains articles written by Memmi, and printed material he collected and annotated, likely for research on topics including international relations, Islam, racism, Latin American politics, Africa, youth and the suburbs, and French Muslims from Algeria.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Not arranged in any particular order.

did.unittitle: L'Action [photocopies of articles by Memmi in 1956-1957]
Articles by Albert Memmi on racism and apartheid, published in various languages
Articles by Albert Memmi on sociology and Judeity
Articles and clippings on diverse subjects
Articles and clippings on diverse subjects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of writings by others on Memmi, articles and interviews by Jacques Howlett and Jacqueline Leiner, and a draft of a book by René Pommier.

did.unittitle: Articles, essays, and other writings on Albert Memmi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and typescripts of works by Afifa and Samir Marzouki and others, sometimes annotated by Memmi.

did.unittitle: Howlett, Jacques. "A l'identité culturelle en question et les langues maternelles ou la parole volée," offprint of Présence africaine inscribed by the author

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescripts, proofs, offprints, and photocopies of interviews of Aimé Césaire, Camara Laye, and Léon Damas by Jacqueline Leiner, as well as articles by Leiner on various topics.

did.unittitle: Pommier, René. Rire et colère d'un incroyant

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of Albert Memmi's correspondence with colleagues, friends, publishers and editors, and fans, among them scholars and writers associated with the Négritude movement such as Léopold Sédar Senghor and Alioune Diop, and Tunisian, Israeli, and French politicians, such as Mohamed Sayah, Francois Mitterand, Mohamed Mzali, Menachem Begin, Jacques Chirac, and Shimon Peres. Other correspondents include James Baldwin, Simone de Beauvoir, André Chouraqui, Driss Chraïbi, Jean Duvignaud, Vladimir Jankélévitch, and Dominique Noguez, and organizations such as Amnesty International, Association francophone d'amitié et de liaison, Mouvement contre le racisme et pour l'amitié entre les peuples, Organisation internationale de la Francophonie, Union des étudiants juifs de France, and the World Jewish Congress. The series also contains correspondence with translators or scholars of Memmi's work such as Guy Dugas, Howard Greenfeld, Édouard Roditi, Michèle Robequain, and Judith Roumani, and letters from Memmi's family including his sister Mado, brother Igal, mother Maïra, and son Daniel.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Alphabetical Correspondence and Chronological Correspondence.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated Materials: Correspondence can also be found in Series I. Writings, and Series IV. Professional and Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries primarily consists of correspondence with public figures described as "notable" by Memmi, such as Taos Amrouche, Élisabeth Badinter, James Baldwin, Simone de Beauvoir, Gwendolyn Brooks, Albert Camus, Mark Chagall, Alioune Diop, Claude Lévi-Strauss, Darius Milhaud, Shimon Peres, Edgar Pisani, Jean-Paul Sartre, and Elie Wiesel. Also included is correspondence with Léopold Sédar Senghor, the Académie française, Guy Dugas, Pierre Maillot, and Michèle Robequain, author of an unrealized biography of Memmi, as well as readers' letters and testimonials. Additional letters from "notable" correspondents and copies of Memmi's letters to Baldwin, Camus, and others, are located in the Chronological Correspondence subseries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters and documents related to the word "judeity" and the dictionary of the French Academy, as well as correspondence regarding Memmi's candidature for the Academy.

did.unittitle: Condolences on the death of Germaine Memmi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, contracts, and other documents related to publishing books and articles by Memmi.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Jean Amrouche, Taos Amrouche (also known as Marguerite-Taos), Aragon, Jacqueline Arnaud, Gabriel Audisio, Dominique Aury, James Baldwin, Princess Marie Bonaparte, Fernand Braudel, Martin Buber, Jean Blot, Roger Caillois, Albert Camus, Jean Cassou, Driss Chraïbi, André Chouraqui, Mohammed Dib, Jean Duvignaud, René Etiemble, Jean Genet, Vladimir Jankélévitch, Daniel Lagache, Emmanuel Levinas, Claude Lévi-Strauss, Matta, François Mauriac, Pierre Mendès-France, Darius Milhaud, Edgar Morin, Maurice Nadeau, Dominique Noguez, Aimé Patri, Alain Peyrefitte, Henri Pichette, Edgard Pisani, Maurice Politi, Raymond Queneau, Jean-François Revel, Marthe Robert, Emmanuel Roblès, Édouard Roditi, Claude Roy, Jules Roy, Jean Sénac, André Spire, Phạm Văn Ký, and Max Zweig.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Simone de Beauvoir, Menachem Begin, Jean-Louis Bory, Willy Brandt, Fernand Braudel, Gwendolyn Brooks, Charlotte Bruner, Michel Butor, Roger Caillois, Marc Chagall, André Chouraqui, Driss Chraïbi, Jean-Emmanuel Clancier, Maurice Clavel, Albert Cohen, Alioune Diop, Jean Daniel, René Etiemble, Pierre Emmanuel, Michel Foucault, Françoise Giroud, Marek Halter, Jean Hélion, Roger Ikor, Eugène Ionesco, Vladimir Jankélévitch, Armand Lanoux, Claude Lévi-Strauss, Amadou-Mahtar M'Bow, Pierre Mendès-France, François Mitterand, Yves Navarre, Dominique Noguez, François Nourrissier, Jean d'Ormesson, Aimé Patri, Shimon Peres, Edgard Pisani, Alain Peyrefitte, Léon Poliakov, Marthe Robert, Claude Roy, Jean-Paul Sartre, Mohamed Sayah, Jean Sénac, Txillardegi (José Luis Álvarez Enparantza), Simone Veil, Vercors (pseudonym of Jean Marcel Bruller), Elie Wiesel, and André Wurmser.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Mouloud Aounit, Jacques Attali, Albert Bensoussan, Jacques Chirac, André Comte-Sponville, Jean Daniel, Pierre Emmanuel, René Etiemble, Laurent Fabius, Edgar Faure, Jack Lang, Claude Lévy-Strauss, Joseph Losey, Mohamed Mzali, François Mitterand, Yves Navarre, Shimon Peres, Alain Peyrefitte, Edgard Pisani, Henri Raczymow, Maurice Rheims, Claude Roy, Georges Sarre, Simone Veil, Vercors, Elie Wiesel, and André Wurmser.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Mouloud Aounit, Jacques Attali, Albert Bensoussan, Jacques Chirac, André Comte-Sponville, Jean Daniel, Pierre Emmanuel, René Etiemble, Laurent Fabius, Edgar Faure, Jack Lang, Claude Lévy-Strauss, Joseph Losey, Mohamed Mzali, François Mitterand, Yves Navarre, Shimon Peres, Alain Peyrefitte, Edgard Pisani, Henri Raczymow, Maurice Rheims, Claude Roy, Georges Sarre, Simone Veil, Vercors, Elie Wiesel, and André Wurmser.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Jacques Attali, Élisabeth Badinter, Robert Badinter, Élie Barnavi, Mongi Bousnina, Pierre Cardin, André Chouraqui, Abdelhamid Escheikh, Luc Ferry, Guy Hennebelle, Moncef Khemiri, Serge Moati, Dominique Noguez, Shimon Peres, Alain Peyrefitte, Jean-François Revel, Georges Sarre, Shmuel Trigano, and Simone Veil.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Henri Atlan, Nefissa Ayachi, Dominique Bourel, Jean Louis Debré, Maurice Druon, M'hamed Hassine Fantar, Pierre Nora, Michel Onfray, Jean-Guy Talamoni, Alek Baylee Toumi, and Nissim Zvili.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include Le Portrait du colonisé , L'Homme dominé , and projects related to "Négritude and Judeïté."

did.unittitle: Tributes and testimonials - original letters, photocopies, and printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Howard Greenfeld, Elisabeth Huppert, Serge Richard, Bzahim Najai, Alessio Loreti, Peter Cairns, and Helena Obolensky.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Among the correspondents in the 1950s and 1960s are French critics Jacqueline Arnaud and Jean Wagner; Tunisian-French journalist Béchir Ben Yahmed; French journalist and translator Henry Bulawko; the Centre militaire d'information et de spécialisation pour l'outre-mer; French librarian Jean Déjeux; Israeli author and politician Avraham Elmalih; French authors Lucie Faure and Jeanne Ranay; American essayist Clement Greenberg; French sociologist Georges Gurvitch; Nigerian literary scholar Francis Abiola Irele; Israeli author Jacqueline Kahanoff; Moroccan poet and activist Abdellatif Laâbi; French publisher and former military officer Jacques Lazarus; French jurist and activist Léon Lyon-Caen; Mazisi (Raymond) Kunene and the African National Congress; Moroccan writer Victor Malka; Algerian writer Kaddour M'Hamsadji; Polish-born poet Joseph Milbauer; French politician Lucien Paye; American writer and editor Norman Podhoretz; Italian translator Adriana Salem and her husband, Greek mathematician Raphaël Salem; French-Tunisian sociologist and historian Paul Sebag; French historian Lilly Scherr; and French philosopher Jean Wahl.

Correspondents in the 1970s include Israeli diplomat Asher Ben-Natan, French journalist and politician Jean-Pierre Biondi, American author and translator Charlotte Bruner, Tunisian writer Majid El Houssi, United States Senator Philip A. Hart, Organizacion Sionista Argentina, and American scholar Paulette J. Trout, in addition to Don Peretz, Simha Flapan, and other scholars from American universities proposing establish a Middle East Research Project to support study of the Arab-Israeli conflict. Correspondents in the 1980s include Haitian poet and activist René Depestre, French historian Ely Ben-Gal, French politicians Jack Lang and Louis Périllier, and American film and television producer David Susskind.

Letters to Memmi in the 1990s and 2000s are from correspondents such as French fashion designer Pierre Cardin; French historian and archaeologist Gilbert Charles-Picard; French authors Suzanne Blaise, Denis Clair, Caroline Fourest, Lucette Heller, and Pierre Maillot; Senegalese politician Abdou Diouf; the French Academy; French psychoanalyst André Green; French mathematician Jean-Pierre Kahane; Congolese writer Kama Sywor Kamanda; Franco-Swedish writer Claude Kayat; Spanish psychoanalyst Marie Rose Moro; French historian Claude Nataf; Tunisian politician Hédi Saïdi; American professor Mel B. Yoken; and Israeli politician Nissim Zvili.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from Nora Blakely, Gwendolyn Brooks's daughter, then visiting France.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series primarily consists of portraits of Albert Memmi and photographs of Memmi during lectures, awards ceremonies, debates, and travels in Africa, Israel, Tunisia, Italy, the United States, and other regions, as well as family and personal photographs. Included are images of pre-World War II Tunis and postwar Tunisia, and of the Jewish quarter of the Tunis. The series also contains a small amount of negatives, contact sheets, and slides.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits of Memmi from the time of his early youth, photographs of family and vacations, postwar Tunisia, and Claude Roy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Memmi in Argentina, Seattle, and at the University of Washington, where he was lecturing students.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Memmi and Blaise Compaoré, president of Burkina Faso, when Memmi received the Ordre du Mérite des Arts, Lettres et de la Communication du Burkina-Faso, as well as Memmi with friends and colleagues such as Claude Sitbon and Rashid Boudjedera.

did.unittitle: Dedication ceremony of the "Words and Images" literary project at Ben-Gurion University of the Negrev [album]
Albert Memmi portrait by Jacques Robert, mounted on cardboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials related to Albert Memmi's education, early writings, and career as a professor and scholar at École pratique des hautes études, École des hautes études commerciales de Paris, University of Nanterre, and Centre national de la recherche scientifique, as well as biographical information, bibliographies, awards and certificates, and inscriptions to Memmi from other authors and public figures. Also included are materials documenting publication of Memmi's books and articles, such as correspondence, contracts, and financial records from Gallimard and other publishing houses, and files of clippings, readers' letters, and ephemera compiled by press agencies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, administrative documents, requests related to vacations, promotions, honorary appointments, financial records, pension application, curriculum vitae, and teaching reports.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes French or foreign awards such as Chevalier des Palmes académiques (1970), Chevalier de la Légion d'honneur (1980); Officier de l'ordre de la République tunisienne (1984); Distinction décernée par la Fédération Internationale des Juifs Humanistes et Laïcs (1986); Titre de docteur honoris causa en philosophie de l'Université Ben-Gourion du Néguev (1999); Ordre du Mérite des Arts, Lettres et de la Communication du Burkina-Faso (2000); Prix de l'Afrique méditerranéenne / Maghreb de l'Association des Écrivains de Langue française (2002); and Grand Prix de la Francophonie de l'Académie française (2004).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to Memmi's life and career, including notes handwritten by Guy Dugas or Michèle Robequain, correspondence, form letters, curriculum vitae, writings, conference and lecture materials, clippings, ephemera, and a few photographs.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, certificates, report cards, diplomas, and other school papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report cards, identification cards, and other records related to schools attended by Memmi, as well as faculty positions for which he applied in the 1950s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes taken during lectures by the philosopher Daniel Lagache.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: More than 350 inscriptions to Memmi, on cut out title or half-title pages, from individuals including Jean Amrouche, Henri Amouroux, Louis Aragon, Georges Auclair, Jean Baudrillart, Helé Béji, Tahar Ben Jelloun, Albert Bensoussan, Jean Blot, Pierre de Boisdeffre, Jean-Louis Bory, Alain Bosquet, Jean de Boschère, Jacques de Bourbon-Busset, Serge Bramly, Jean-Jacques Brochier, Gwendolyn Brooks, Albert Camus, Albert Caraco, Andrée Chedid, Bernard Clavel, Régis Debray, Jean Duvignaud, René Etiemble, Lucie Faure, Max-Pol Fouchet, Bernard Franck, Edouard Glissant, Louis Guilloux, Marek Haddad, Jean Hélion, Roger Ikor, Ionesco, Vladimir Jankélévitch, Francis Jourdain, Edmond Jouve, Jacques Lanzmann, Meyer Levin, Clara Malraux, Jacqueline Mesnil-Amar, Serge Moati, Florette Morand, Yves Navarre, Pierre Nora, Pierre Paraz, Aimé Patri, René Pommier, Henri Rackzymov, Jean-François Revel, Emmanuel Roblès, Jacqueline de Romilly, Claude Roy, Nathalie Sarraute, Jean Sénac, Jorge Semprun, Jean-Louis Servan-Schreiber, Antoine Spire, Vercors, Françoise Vergès, and Elie Wiesel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, notes, financial records, and other documents related to Memmi's publishers, including Arléa, Buchet-Chastel, Grasset, Julliard, Maspero, and Seghers, as well as various audiovisual projects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, notes, financial records, contracts, and other documents related to the publisher Gallimard and intellectual property assignment.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of clippings, ephemera, correspondence, and other materials compiled on Memmi's behalf by various press agencies in Paris.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of clippings, ephemera, correspondence, and other materials compiled on Memmi's behalf by various press agencies in Paris.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of clippings, ephemera, correspondence, and other materials compiled on Memmi's behalf by various press agencies in Paris.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of clippings, ephemera, correspondence, and other materials compiled on Memmi's behalf by various press agencies in Paris.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings, correspondence, notes, and financial records for articles by Memmi published in the newspaper La Tribune de Genève .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains floppy disks, CDs and DVDs, videocassettes, and audiocassettes containing interviews of Albert Memmi, documentaries, and recordings of events, as well as some material related to Memmi's writings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media and Audiovisual Media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of computer files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two subheadings: Audiocassettes and Videocassettes. Within each subheading, items are arranged alphabetically by title.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
